header: From composer require to a themed site in 30 minutes
description: Four steps, no configuration files, no license keys. Or skip all four: the creators install and set up Desiderio on your TYPO3 site for a flat €890.

items.title: Install the package
items.content: Run composer require webconsulting/desiderio in a TYPO3 14.3 project. The extension registers all 244 Content Blocks elements, the Fluid 5 component library, and the theme layer automatically — there is no step two of the template project, because there is no template project.

image.title: Developer installing Desiderio
image.alternative: Hands typing on a laptop keyboard during a command-line installation.
image.description: Copyright/credit: Photo by Glenn Carstens-Peters on Unsplash. Used as seeded demo imagery.

items.title: Seed the styleguide
items.content: One command — vendor/bin/typo3 desiderio:styleguide:seed — builds a complete demo page tree with every element. Workspaces-safe and idempotent, so re-running never creates duplicates. You'll have something to show a client before the coffee is cold.

image.title: Desiderio page module previews
image.alternative: TYPO3 page module showing seeded Desiderio content elements with rendered backend previews.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.

items.title: Pick a theme preset
items.content: Choose one of 15 shadcn presets in site settings, or assign a different preset to any page subtree — the seeded styleguide flaunts a different theme on every chapter page. Dark mode and WCAG 2.2-checked contrast come included with every single one.

image.title: Desiderio site settings theme category
image.alternative: TYPO3 site settings module showing the Desiderio theme preset selection.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.

items.title: Build your pages
items.content: Open the new content element wizard and compose pages from the ten element groups. Every block has a backend preview and seeded example content to start from — your editors are productive on day one, not after a training week.

image.title: Desiderio new content element wizard
image.alternative: TYPO3 new content element wizard listing all ten Desiderio element groups.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.
